Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Simple Basic attackers from Operation Wocao target energy sector using phishing to gain initial access, execute malicious files, and cause impact through data destruction.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Reg.exe Utility, File Modification

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-4762: Type confusion vulnerability in Chromium-based browsers allowing remote code execution.
stage.description: Attackers send phishing emails to trick employees into clicking on a link or opening an attachment leading to credential theft or malware download.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2017-6862: Buffer overflow in NETGEAR devices enabling authentication bypass and RCE.
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: A malicious file attached to the phishing email is executed by the user using reg.exe utility, executing unauthorized commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: After gaining execution privileges, attackers modify or delete critical files causing data destruction within the system.

Scenario.summary: Operation Wocao employs basic phishing techniques to compromise internet-facing assets of energy companies, leading to the execution of malicious files and subsequent data destruction.
